title: Police file FIR against viral video of pro-Pakistan posters put up in J&K’s Doda
content: Posters praising Pakistani PM, Army Chief’s role in U.S.-Iran peace talks were pasted on a Doda bridge;  police trying to verify origin, authenticity of viral video

title: Alleged drug trafficking kingpin deported from Türkiye to India
content: 59-year-old accused Salim Dola was handed over to the Narcotics Control Bureau and taken to its headquarters in Delhi

title: A grief divided: funeral held on the Kishanganga’s banks prompts calls to reopen LoC crossing points
content: Siblings of the dead Kupwara resident, who have lived in POK since 1989, bid farewell to their brother from across the river; LoC crossing points used by such divided families were shut by the Centre in 2019

title: West Bengal goes to the polls in the second phase of Assembly election
content: The 142 seats going to the polls in the second phase are spread across seven districts of south Bengal, including Kolkata; about 3.21 crore electors will determine the fate of 1,448 candidates in the fray

title: Police arrest ‘ISI-backed Khalistanis who triggered low intensity bomb on railway track’
content: Interrogation revealed that the accused conducted a low-intensity IED blast at the main line of the Shambhu railway station

title: IPL 2026 RR vs PBKS: Stoinis powers PBKS to 222 for 4 vs Rajasthan Royals
content: Table toppers Punjab too made two changes with Shashank Singh and Xavier Bartlett making way for Suryansh Shedge and Lockie Ferguson

title: Watch: UAE shocks world with dramatic OPEC exit | Above the Fold | 28.04.2026
content: From the UAE’s OPEC exit to India’s heatwave, SCO meet updates and the Jakarta train crash, we bring you the top stories that matter with clarity and context.

title: Karnataka High Court stays CERC’s new regulations imposing higher penalty for deviation on renewable energy firms
content: The petitioners argued that the new framework is draconian and scientifically unfounded, given the inherently unpredictable, weather-dependent nature of solar and wind power generation

title: Stalin stands exposed after PM Modi’s assurance on delimitation: Edappadi Palaniswami
content: Pointing out that the share of Tamil Nadu in the Lok Sabha would go up from 7.18% to 7.23%, Mr. Palaniswami, in an interview with The Hindu, said that “this disproves what he [Mr. Stalin] has been saying.”

title: Rahul Gandhi reaches Great Nicobar, says he made it despite administration’s efforts to stop him
content: The Lok Sabha LoP met with Nicobarese community leaders resisting the Union government’s mega infrastructure project, settler and ex-servicemen families, and paid floral tributes to former PM Indira Gandhi at Indira Point

title: Future City will have global standards: Revanth
content: ‘Government committed to protecting the interests of farmers, who gave their land for the project; office of Future City Development Corporation will be inaugurated on June 2, coinciding with Telangana Formation Day, accelerating the development of the area.

title: Supreme Court asks amicus curiae to meet States, UTs on Central funds for CCTVs in police stations
content: Hearing based on court’s suo motu cognisance of the issue of video coverage of police stations, especially in lock-up areas and interrogation rooms, to prevent custodial torture

title: BJD urges EC to disclose action taken on its Rajya Sabha polling complaint
content: The issuance of second ballot papers to BJP MLAs Upasana Mohapatra and Purna Chandra Sethy in the March 16 Rajya Sabha election is illegal, the regional party alleges

title: Devotees throng Meenakshi Sundareswarar Temple to witness celestial wedding
content: The ‘Thirukalyanam’, the highlight of the annual Chithirai festival, took place at the flower-bedecked Thirukalyana Mandapam  between 8.35 a.m. and 8.59 a.m.

title: Harsh sun leaves traditional fishers in distress
content: Searing heat drives fish to deeper, cooler waters, escalating the cost of fishing. Warming waters lead to a surge in population of jellyfish, which tear nets and cause allergies to fishers. The working hours come down, the catch gets spoiled faster, and sun leaves the fish vendors perennially exhausted

title: Police recruitment will resume as quota issue is resolved, says Home Minister
content: He was speaking after receiving a ceremonial salute at a passing-out parade of probationers at the Police Training College in Naganahalli

title: Norms must govern places of worship or risk ‘anarchy’, Chishti–Nizami descendant says in Sabarimala case
content: Syed Altamash Nizami says the faith of a believer cannot yield to the whim of a third-party interloper or a tourist to walk into the sanctum sanctorum

title: Nitish Kumar’s son Nishant to begin his political career with Sadbhav Yatra from West Champaran
content: Janata Dal (United) Bihar unit president Umesh Singh Kushwaha says the Yatra will shape the direction of the State politics for the next three-to-four decades.

title: Officials urged to enrol migrant, fishing community children in schools, hostels
content: State Commission for Protection of Child Rights member Gandhi Babu inspects anganwadi schools and PHCs in Eluru district and interact with the children, parents and the staff

title: Advertorial on Chennithala: Kerala Pradesh Congress Committee issues show-cause notice to Prajin Babu
content: Both the All India Congress Committee and the Kerala Pradesh Congress Committee had previously directed that Congress leaders and workers should not make public statements in print, visual, or social media or any other platform in a manner that brings disrepute to the organisation

title: Sajjala alleges ‘artificial’ fuel scarcity in Andhra Pradesh
content: YSRCP leader blames coalition government for supply disruption, says transport and public life hit across the State

title: Union Minister Chouhan earns praise of J&K CM as Centre clears ₹3,566-crore PMGSY package
content: J&K Chief Minister Omar Abdullah says move will help connect 363 habitations through 330 roads, covering approximately 1,600 kilometres, significantly enhancing last-mile connectivity and driving more inclusive and equitable development across our region.

title: Assembly election 2026 highlights: Tribunal clears 1,468 electors to vote in second phase of polls in West Bengal, says official
content: According to the Election Commission, voters whose names appear in the supplementary roll can check details by entering their EPIC number on its website

title: PAV opposes PPP model for operation of four government medical colleges
content: The government decision to hand over Markapuram, Madanapalle, Pulivendula, and Adoni government medical colleges to private entities in the first phase is deeply concerning, says Praja Arogya Vedika

title: After objections, Hosaritti society decides to auction property
content: The management members have now decided to withdraw their earlier decision and also, those who have purchased sites will return them

title: Threat or theater? Trump assassination bid case turns into political noir
content: US authorities have charged a suspect in an alleged third assassination attempt on President Trump during a White House Correspondents' dinner security breach. The incident has polarized the nation, with conservatives citing escalating political violence and liberals expressing skepticism. Late-night hosts' jokes have further fueled tensions, prompting White House calls for regulatory scrutiny of networks.

title: US-Iran war impact on India: Why RBI is cautious yet confident
content: For RBI the situation is clear: The resilience of the global economy, already inflicted with trade tensions, is being tested by the conflict in West Asia. The biggest takeaway from the RBI bulletin is that India’s domestic supply chains may come under risk from a prolonged war scenario, though strong macroeconomic fundamentals provide a buffer.

title: Is US leaving Nato? Why Spain and Iran war could trigger Trump’s biggest global shock
content: Fresh reports reveal internal Pentagon discussions exploring punitive measures against Nato allies, including Spain, for perceived lack of support during the Iran conflict. These discussions, alongside President Trump's continued questioning of US commitment, expose deep divisions within the alliance, raising questions about its future and European security.

title: The Phase 2 trust vote in West Bengal: Who will win the Matua votes?
content: Electoral analysts estimate that Matua voters influence outcomes in 60 to 65 assembly seats. In the second phase alone, they are a key factor in 21 constituencies. In tightly contested elections, this scale of influence gives the community leverage that political parties cannot ignore. This is evident in recent electoral trends.

title: China gives two week deadline to Mark Zuckerberg’s Meta to abort $2bn Manus deal
content: China has ordered Meta to reverse its $2.5 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus within weeks, citing national security. Beijing claims authority over the Singapore-based firm's China-linked AI technology, demanding the return of assets and data. This move signals a strong warning to Chinese tech founders against exporting sensitive innovations to geopolitical rivals.

title: Rohit Sharma injury update: MI keep fans guessing ahead of SRH clash at Wankhede
content: Rohit Sharma's participation in Mumbai Indians' IPL 2026 match against Sunrisers Hyderabad is uncertain. A final decision will be made at the toss. Sharma has been recovering from a hamstring injury. Suryakumar Yadav is preparing intensely for the game. Players from both teams were seen interacting during training sessions. Mumbai Indians await a potential boost for this key home contest.

title: Mamata's big test: Will brand 'Didi' deliver again in Bengal?
content: Mamata Banerjee's 'Didi' brand faces its toughest test in West Bengal's elections, balancing welfare schemes and grassroots appeal against BJP's national push.  Despite corruption allegations and voter fatigue, her maternalist state model and street-fighter persona remain key, as she rallies voters against perceived disenfranchisement and external forces.

title: After $6.25 bn 'gift' to 'Trump Accounts', Michael & Susan Dell donate $750 mn
content: Michael and Susan Dell have donated $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin for a new medical center and research campus. This facility will integrate AI and advanced computing to improve patient care, research, and education, with construction beginning this year and opening by 2030. The donation builds on their extensive philanthropic history and commitment to Austin's growth.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 41: Ranveer Singh film eyes 9 year record of 'Baahubali 2'
content: On April 28, 2026, ‘Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge’ reached a worldwide gross of Rs. 1,779.02 crore, placing it just Rs. 10.54 crore away from beating ‘Baahubali 2’ to become the second highest-grossing Indian film globally. Directed by Aditya Dhar and starring Ranveer Singh, the film’s India net stands at Rs. 1,131.83 crore, continuing its pursuit of ‘Pushpa 2’s domestic record. Despite the IPL 2026 season and the release of ‘Bhooth Bangla,’ the espionage thriller remains a box office juggernaut.

title: Koovagam festival: Where transgenders flock to wed, celebrate and mourn - photos
content: Thousands of transgender women, dressed in fine silk saris and adorned with traditional jewellery and flowers, participated in a symbolic mass wedding.

title: IPL 2026: Why Punjab Kings wore black armbands against Rajasthan Royals
content: Punjab Kings players donned black armbands against Rajasthan Royals, honouring teammate Azmatullah Omarzai's late mother. Captain Shreyas Iyer conveyed heartfelt condolences, emphasising team solidarity. Punjab posted a formidable 222/4, showcasing resilience and unity in a high-scoring IPL 2026 encounter.

title: Scott Bessent slams Bernie Sanders over AI invite; ‘Stop inviting foreign nationals’
content: A political clash has ignited online between US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Senator Bernie Sanders regarding the inclusion of Chinese scientists in a crucial AI safety forum. Sanders advocates for international cooperation to address AI's existential threat, while Bessent criticizes the move, asserting American AI leadership and warning against foreign influence on US regulation.

title: 'Politics has its place': Akhilesh visits BJP MLA who was injured while burning his effigy
content: SP leader Akhilesh Yadav visited BJP MLA Anupama Jaiswal in a Lucknow hospital after she sustained severe facial burns while attempting to set fire to an effigy. Yadav expressed his wishes for her speedy recovery, emphasizing that political differences should not overshadow compassion and human relationships. Jaiswal's husband lauded the visit as an act of "human sensitivity and democratic courtesy."

title: Mirzapur film: Divyenndu promises a full paisa vasool ride
content: Mirzapur: The Film is easily among the most anticipated projects of the year, and the excitement has only intensified with Divyenndu Sharma returning as Munna Bhaiya. With fans closely tracking every update, the actor has now shared a glimpse of what’s in store—and it sounds massive.

title: Mike Vrabel divorce buzz grows amid Russini scandal, Patriots return
content: Mike Vrabel returned to the New England Patriots after missing part of the draft, but attention has shifted to his personal life. Speculation about his marriage to Jen Vrabel intensified after comments from Jason Whitlock predicting a split. While Vrabel emphasized accountability and family in his statement, no divorce has been confirmed, leaving his situation surrounded by growing public scrutiny and debate.

title: Auston Matthews, Nylander push Leafs to fix roster gaps urgently
content: Auston Matthews and William Nylander have reportedly urged the Toronto Maple Leafs to make improvements in key areas ahead of the next NHL season. They want to add physical forwards and mobile defensemen. The plea points to the internal unease after a disappointing campaign and the potential for changes in the offseason to the roster and team strategy.

title: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce plan star-studded wedding amid privacy push
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce live a life full of luxury and it seems their wedding will be no less as billions wait for it. As the high profile couple gear up for the biggest day of their lives, it seems sky is the limit when it comes to spending money on their wedding.

title: Watch: Virat Kohli fan breaks down after failing to get autograph in viral video
content: A young Virat Kohli fan's heartbreaking reaction to a missed autograph opportunity outside the RCB hotel has gone viral during IPL 2026. The boy's tearful outburst after being unable to meet his idol highlights the immense fan adoration for the star India batter, who is also enjoying a record-breaking season.

title: Chris Simms on Russini-Vrabel buzz as Mike Tomlin takes NBC Sports job
content: An alleged Sedona cheating scandal involving top NFL reporter Dianna Russini and New England Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel has led to significant fallout in the media industry. Russini resigned from The Athletic, Crisy Froyd was fired for extending her support to Russini, and Chris Simms lost his NBC Sports job after impatiently hinting at the story. Interestingly, Pittsburgh Steelers longtime head coach Mike Tomlin, who previously granted Russini interview during COVID-19 restrictions has now joined NBC Sports in place of Simms.

title: Greece: 89-yr-old held after shootings at social security office, courthouse, 4 injured
content: An 89-year-old man was apprehended in Patras after allegedly injuring at least four people in two separate shootings in Athens. Authorities believe pension grievances and psychological issues fueled the attacks, which targeted a social security office and a court building. The suspect, found with a weapon, is under investigation.

title: Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused of killing husband during honeymoon gets bail
content: A Meghalaya court granted bail to Sonam Raghuvanshi, accused in the 2025 honeymoon killing of her husband, Raja Raghuvanshi. His mutilated body was found in June last year. Police had filed a charge sheet naming eight individuals, including Sonam and her alleged boyfriend, in connection with the transport businessman's death.

title: UAE to exit OPEC, OPEC+ amid Hormuz disruptions: What it means for world oil order
content: The Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries or OPEC is a group of major oil-producing nations. This group coordinates their petroleum production policies to help effectively manage global oil supply and influence crude prices.

title: 'If he's Singham, I am Pushpa': TMC protests over IPS Ajay Sharma's warning on voter intimidation
content: Trinamool Congress supporters in West Bengal's South 24 Parganas protested against an Election Commission-appointed police observer, Ajay Pal Sharma. They accused him of intimidating party workers ahead of the second phase of polling. Sharma had warned of strict action against those influencing voters, prompting a strong response from TMC candidate Jahangir Khan.

title: Rani Kapur reacts as SC urges mediation in Rs 30,000 crore dispute
content: The Supreme Court urged mediation in the estate dispute of late businessman Sanjay Kapur, noting his mother Rani Kapur's age. Rani welcomed the court's suggestion for a peaceful resolution, seeking truth and protection of her rightful assets. The court emphasized mediation as a better alternative to prolonged legal battles.

title: Watch: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi wins heart with humble gesture for young fan
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, fresh off a century against Sunrisers Hyderabad, arrived at the Rajasthan Royals team hotel. Despite his recent success, he displayed humility by gifting his welcome garland to a young fan.  The team is preparing to face Punjab Kings in Mullanpur.

title: IPL 2026: PBKS unleash Lockie Ferguson as pace weapon; RR opt to bowl
content: Punjab Kings bolstered their bowling with the formidable Lockie Ferguson for their IPL 2026 clash against Rajasthan Royals.  Rajasthan won the toss and chose to bowl, aiming to exploit early conditions.  Unbeaten Punjab seek to extend their winning streak, while fourth-placed Rajasthan eye a climb up the table with a strong performance.

title: MEA condemns Mali terror attacks, calls for regional stability
content: India has strongly condemned the "terrorist attacks" in Mali, extending condolences to victims and expressing solidarity with the nation. The Ministry of External Affairs also mourned the death of Mali's Defence Minister in the assault. This coordinated militant offensive, the largest in over a decade, has significantly worsened security, despite Russian support for the military government.

title: ‘Trusting batters down the order’: Manjrekar reveals key to Kohli’s transformation
content: Virat Kohli's T20 batting transformation this IPL season is attributed to a key realisation: he's no longer indispensable.  Sanjay Manjrekar notes Kohli now bats quicker, trusting teammates and abandoning the anchor role. This shift, boosting his strike rate significantly, has unlocked RCB's potential, proving modern T20 demands aggression over individual longevity.

title: Remo, wife Lizelle on child before marriage and wedding journey
content: Choreographer-director Remo D'Souza and his wife Lizelle shared their unconventional love story, revealing they lived together and had their first son before marriage. Despite parental disapproval and societal taboos, they navigated parenthood and eventually married, celebrating their union multiple times over 25 years.

title: Maharashtra IS-inspired attack: 'Suspect had desire to attack Hindus,' CM Fadnavis says
content: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis stated a "lone wolf" stabbing of two security guards in Thane is being investigated as potential self-radicalisation. The accused, who recently returned from the US, allegedly targeted Hindus in the name of jihad. Investigators are exploring wider networks, with the ATS and NIA involved. Recovered notes suggest a desire to join ISIS.

title: ‘They said I’d never fix it’: Shreyas reveals how short-ball criticism ‘triggered’ him
content: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer revealed that relentless criticism about his short-ball struggles fueled a dramatic batting transformation in IPL 2026.  He admitted that doubts triggered a shift in mindset and preparation, leading him to aggressively attack short deliveries. This improved control against pace has been crucial to Punjab's unbeaten streak, including a record-breaking chase.

title: Gujarat civic polls: BJP sweeps all 15 corporations, crosses 50% vote share across the board
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party has achieved a significant win across all municipal corporations. This victory occurred in the home regions of Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Union Home Minister Amit Shah. The saffron party surpassed the 50% mark in every corporation. These civic elections saw a large voter turnout.

title: Sooryavanshi-Yashasvi vs Arya-Prabhsimran: Who holds edge in battle of openers
content: Yashasvi Jaiswal, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Priyansh Arya, and Prabhsimran Singh have all been in brilliant form this season​, and the contest is being viewed as a battle between two opening pairs.

title: Bengal exit polls: All eyes on Mamata vs BJP — date, timing, when and where to watch
content: West Bengal's crucial assembly elections are nearing their conclusion, with exit polls set to release on April 29 and final results on May 4.  The state witnessed record voter turnout in Phase 1, and Phase 2, covering key strongholds including Mamata Banerjee's Bhabanipur constituency, is now underway.  Prominent leaders from TMC and BJP are in contention.

title: 'Tough to take': Delhi co-owner breaks silence after DC all out for 75 vs RCB
content: Josh Hazlewood took four wickets and Bhuvneshwar Kumar picked up three as Delhi were bowled out for just 75.

title: Video: Fire erupts at Russian refinery after Ukraine drone attack, nearby buildings evacuated
content: A Ukrainian drone strike ignited a massive fire at Russia's Tuapse oil refinery on the Black Sea, prompting evacuations. This major Rosneft facility, crucial for fuel exports, had previously halted operations after an earlier drone attack. Authorities are responding to the incident, with the Kremlin confirming measures are being taken.

title: Over 60 injured as overloaded bus overturns on Purvanchal Expressway in UP
content: A speeding luxury bus overturned on the Purvanchal Expressway in Sultanpur, injuring over 60 passengers. The bus, reportedly overloaded and driven by a conductor or helper, was traveling from Ludhiana to Azamgarh. Rescue operations were swift, with critically injured passengers transferred to higher medical facilities.

title: WhatsApp Banned 9,400-Plus Accounts In 12 Weeks Over Digital Arrest Fraud
content: WhatsApp said its investigation found a large number of accounts targeting Indian users were being operated from southeast Asia, particularly Cambodia.

title: Gaming, Rants, Hitler Comparisons: What Trump Event Shooter Posted Online
content: According to CNN, Allen had shared posts comparing Trump to Hitler and even called upon others who criticised his presidency to purchase guns. His activity on social media ranged from posts about video games to angry political messages.

title: Neighbourhood Schools Must Admit Students Without Delay: Supreme Court
content: The bench said earnest implementation can truly be transformative and it is not only a step towards educating young India, but also a substantive measure in securing the preambular objective of 'equality of status'.

title: This Air Defence System Was Op Sindoor Star. Now Another Is On Its Way
content: The timing places the delivery just days after the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, when the S-400 played a major role in countering Pakistan's aerial threats

title: Indore Woman Accused Of Killing Husband During Honeymoon In Meghalaya Gets Bail
content: Sonam had allegedly confessed to conspiring with her lover, Raj Kushwaha, and three others to kill her husband Raja Raghuvanshi

title: Congress Breaches BJP's Founding Bastion In Ahmedabad's Khadia
content: Khadia had remained an undefeated stronghold for the Jan Sangh and subsequently the BJP since the early 1970s

title: Delhi Court Grants Bail To J&K MP Engineer Rashid To Visit Ailing Father
content: The bench further ordered that at least two police officials would accompany him in plainclothes at all times and Engineer Rashid shall not bear the cost of their travel.

title: BJP Office Peon To Corporator: Rameshbhai Bhil Wins Big In Gujarat Local Body Polls
content: His transition from serving tea and managing files for party leaders to becoming an elected Corporator has become a symbol of grassroots political mobility in North Gujarat.

title: BJP Wins All 15 Municipal Corporations In Gujarat, AAP Surat Stronghold Falls
content: The BJP's success also extended into the taluka and district panchayats, though the Congress and AAP managed to find small pockets of support in rural areas

title: Independent Hindu Candidate Wins In Godhra Ward With 100% Muslim Voters
content: Apekshaben Naineshbhai Soni, an independent Hindu woman candidate, has secured a resounding victory from Ward Number 7, a constituency where 100% of the voters belong to the Muslim community.

title: Pro-Pakistan Poster Video Surfaces in J&K's Doda, Probe Launched
content: A purported video clip showing a pro-Pakistan poster pasted on a parapet in the Pul Doda area of Jammu and Kashmir's Doda district has sent security agencies on high alert.

title: No Plan To Hike Petrol, Diesel Prices Despite Iran War Disruptions: Centre
content: While commercial LPG supplies have been partially impacted, availability has been restored to around 70 per cent, with priority being given to critical sectors such as hospitals and educational institutions.

title: Explained: Legal Options Before Arvind Kejriwal, Court After Standoff Over Recusal
content: Arvind Kejriwal and Manish Sisodia have decided to boycott the proceedings before Justice Sharma in Delhi High Court.

title: Taiwan On Alert After Spotting 2 Chinese Warships Near Its Penghu Islands
content: Taiwan has spotted two Chinese warships operating in waters near the Penghu islands in the Taiwan Strait and has sent its own naval and air forces to keep watch, the defence ministry in Taipei said.

title: In Ajit Pawar Crash, Rohit Pawar's 'Black Magic', 'Godman' Claims
content: NCP leader Amol Mitkari claimed in a post on X that local residents had described a ritual allegedly carried out near Pawar's residence in Baramati.

title: 'Self-Blast' To Avoid Capture By Ukraine: N Korea Confirms Rule For Soldiers
content: Speaking at an event to inaugurate a memorial in Pyongyang for North Koreans who have died in Ukraine, Kim Jong Un twice mentioned soldiers who had "self-blasted"

title: After Jyotiraditya Scindia's Onion Advice, Digvijaya Singh's 'Return AC Cars' Jibe
content: Scindia had yesterday claimed that he doesn't use AC and rather keeps an onion in his pocket to beat the heat.

title: Assam Elections 2026: Akhil Gogoi Eyes Sivasagar Win Again But Tough AGP And BJP Contest Awaits
content: Akhil Gogoi seeks to retain his Sivasagar seat in what is shaping up to be a closely contested fight.

title: ‘When you clean your house, few glasses break’: Subrata Thakur’s SIR defence in Matua heartland
content: Subrata Thakur is the grandson of P R Thakur and matua matriarch Baroma Binapani Devi . he is the sitting MLA and BJP candidate for the election . the election is trickier than the earlier ones for him and other BJP leaders .

title: The red string and the widow: Mystery of the missing scientists in US
content: the internet found a bone in the shape of a common denominator that connects it all . by mid-April 2026, the deaths and disappearances of eleven people with ties to US national security had become a full-blown phenomenon . the white house fielded questions; congress demanded answers .
